creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: This talk addresses a critical failure mode in web-accessing agents: they may claim to have searched the web while actually falling back to stale training data due to blocking, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages. The speaker demonstrates the difference between blocked and unblocked agent runs and explains the technical barriers including anti-bot systems, JavaScript rendering, and CAPTCHA handling.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's approach to accelerating video diffusion inference combines quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce denoising steps from fifty to as few as one or four. The techniques are composable, with distribution-based distillation training a student model to match teacher outputs rather than exact trajectories. FastGen packages the post-training and GPU sharding infrastructure to apply these optimizations at scale.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: The double iframe pattern nests an iframe within another iframe to work around Content Security Policy restrictions and sandbox escape vulnerabilities. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the application HTML via srcdoc into the inner frame, preventing storage collisions and unauthorized access to parent application data.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace from WorkOS that allows non-technical users to ask questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The tool generates reusable widgets as declarative JavaScript that call data sources directly, enabling deterministic and efficient subsequent queries without engineering overhead.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that allows users to orchestrate multiple AI coding models, such as Claude Code, Codex, and others, as interchangeable workers. It enables workflows where different models handle different stages of coding tasks, includes governance and human-in-the-loop capabilities, and maintains sessions across multiple devices.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden removal of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 following a US government export-control directive allegedly triggered by a jailbreak. He covers the regulatory action, reasons for the shutdown, and shares benchmarking results comparing Fable 5 to Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integration with AI coding assistants like Claude. The tool enables rapid agent development and deployment workflows.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on Saturdays at 9 AM CT where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines interactive discussion with casual chess gameplay.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video guide on building proactive AI agents capable of autonomous operation and self-improvement through iterative loops. It covers architectural patterns for creating self-improving companies and demonstrates use cases in autonomous ads optimization and SEO.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses advances in autonomous coding agents, featuring the /goal feature and /mission exploration for long-horizon task execution. The video covers practical setup and prompting techniques for AI-assisted development workflows using tools like Crewlet and Superdesign.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding framework that enables automated workflows with harness setup, auto-testing, and workflow configuration. The video demonstrates how Symphony works with codebase tools and testing pipelines.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews several autonomous agent systems and mechanisms, including Autoresearch and AutoAgent frameworks, Claude's 3-layer memory system, Hermes self-evolution mechanism, and OpenClaw skill comparison. The video serves as a comparative overview of agent implementations.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent MoE and MTP techniques for improved performance over standard open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model incorporating MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements that reduce prefill and decode latency by reading cache continuously and efficiently between HBM and SRAM.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is described as a unique agent framework that enables new use cases through its harness layer. The video compares Pi's architecture against frontier agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor for coding tasks.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a response to the semiconductor race between China and the USA. The commentary covers the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing chip manufacturing capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with access to up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates and reduces wasted effort by providing structured knowledge instead of relying on outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers shows how to run Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock for developers with data residency, compliance, or billing constraints. The walkthrough covers setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers addresses job market resilience and career advancement in an AI-driven landscape. The discussion covers networking strategies, personal branding, and dispels fears of junior developer replacement by drawing parallels to historical job evolution in transportation.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: Five production-ready patterns for deploying AI agents in TypeScript: streaming tool call events for responsive UIs, layered authentication combining OAuth and IAM, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, cost and safety guardrails with model routing, and distributed observability via metrics and tracing. These practices address latency, security, data isolation, cost control, and debugging across the agent lifecycle.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video examining whether Google engineers use AI code generation tools, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, and discussing adoption patterns at Google across multiple years (2024-2026).

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source ecosystems. The video explores how Malus operates and its implications for future open source maintainers, framing it as potentially harmful to open source development models.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the focus on humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree may be limiting, and that designing robots for human interaction and delight rather than pure utility will be essential as billions of robots enter society. She suggests that the real challenge in robotics is not achieving intelligence but addressing broader questions about design purpose and human integration.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her startup built an AI performance management platform that consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data. She shares insights on why even innovative companies like Google rely on spreadsheets, the advantages of building a full enterprise platform from day one, and how Pigment won customers by securing trust from respected companies.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid fans and formal customer interviews are poor signals of true product-market fit. He contends that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than push, and advises founders to embed deeply in customer operations while betting on the future direction of AI models rather than their current state.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is superior to subscriptions for AI tools and that human trust, not technology capability, is the primary bottleneck for AI agent adoption. He applies decision frameworks from competitive sailing, emphasizing probability-based thinking and expected value in startup building.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Fable 5 capabilities with a focus on prompt engineering and agentic loops for SaaS applications. The video covers implications of relying on closed-source models versus open-source alternatives and risks in the evolving AI SaaS landscape.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
